summary: End-to-end testing of Coastline PMS across backend (26 pytest cases) and frontend (manager, owner, housekeeper, maintenance flows). All major features pass: auth/role routing, dashboard, properties, maintenance filters + creation, storm activate/standdown (banner + hurricane prep tasks dispatched), inbox + AI draft (real Emergent LLM gpt-5.4, 246-char reply generated), trust accounting (5 split cards + 100+ ledger rows), owner portal (revenue/payout/ROI cards, holds CRUD, maintenance evidence cards), field app (housekeeper EN/ES toggle confirmed switching to Spanish, maintenance cards with before/after/complete buttons, storm tasks with coral styling + checklist).

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking storm-deactivate-btn, the storm-name-input form does not re-render on the dashboard until a full page reload. The dashboard summary state isn't refetched after stand-down.

action_items: Optional: After POST /api/storm/activate (active=false), re-fetch /api/dashboard/summary in ManagerDashboard so the storm activation form re-appears without a page refresh.

Optional: review_request used selectors like 'login-email-input'/'login-submit-btn'/'storm-name-input'; the app uses 'login-email','login-password','login-submit'. Most other testids (storm-name-input, activate-storm-btn, ai-draft-btn, reply-input, send-reply-btn, mt-complete-btn, hk-upload-btn, hk-file-input, add-hold-btn, new-task-btn, lang-toggle, storm-deactivate-btn) match the request exactly. No action needed unless the spec is strict.
critical_code_review_comments: require_roles() automatically allows manager regardless of declared roles — fine for this app's hierarchy but worth documenting.

Storm activation does not roll back the database state when previously-created storm_prep tasks remain after stand-down. They stay 'open' assigned to staff, which is probably intentional (so crews complete prep) but may want a manager UI toggle to bulk-cancel.
AI endpoints have no rate limit or per-thread caching. Manager could spam /api/ai/draft-reply. Consider light debouncing or per-user quota.
/api/auth/login could leak whether email exists vs wrong password via error timing; currently both return same 401 message which is good.
MongoDB '_id' is correctly excluded in all read paths via projection {"_id":0}.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; only minor storm form re-render quirk)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created a few TEST_-prefixed maintenance tasks and an owner hold via existing endpoints. Storm was activated as 'TEST_Hermine' / 'TEST_Idalia' during tests and then stood down via the manager dashboard. Hurricane Prep tasks dispatched to staff (visible in maintenance list).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_coastline_api.py (26 tests) is the regression baseline. Frontend playwright flow verified across all 4 roles via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://coastal-ops.preview.emergentagent.com. AI endpoints take ~5-10s; poll up to 30s. The only minor UI quirk found: storm form (storm-name-input) does not auto re-render in ManagerDashboard after storm stand-down; need full reload.
